summary: The measured ylong benchmark path exercises the public ylong_http_client async ClientBuilder, Proxy, Request, Client::request, connector HTTPS-proxy TLS/tunnel, and response body drain path. The libcurl baseline is a same-process libcurl easy/multi baseline, not curl CLI. This evidence proves real-client-path and fair baseline mechanics; it does not prove SOTA because the threshold matrices still fail.

ylong_public_client_path.evidence: Benchmark imports public async_impl Body, ClientBuilder, and Request from ylong_http_client.
Benchmark imports public Proxy, TimeGroup, TlsConfig, and TlsFileType from ylong_http_client.
build_ylong_client constructs a public async Client through ClientBuilder::new().
For concurrency > 1, the benchmark sets max_h1_conn_number to the requested benchmark concurrency so ylong and libcurl can use matching HTTP/1 connection capacity.
Origin TLS config, public Proxy::all(proxy).tls_config(proxy_tls).build(), and ClientBuilder::build() construct the measured ylong client.
Every measured ylong request constructs Request::builder().url(url).body(Body::empty()) and calls client.request(request).await.
Every measured ylong response is drained to EOF through response.body_mut().data(&mut buf).await, with body chunk and byte counters recorded.
The fair single-client concurrency model shares one public async Client across workers rather than constructing one client per request.
SharedRequestBudget dynamically issues one request at a time, matching libcurl multi's complete-and-refill benchmark semantics more closely than fixed partitions.
The public async Client::request method delegates into the normal client send_request path.
The public ClientBuilder::proxy method installs the Proxy into the client proxy set.
The public ClientBuilder::build method constructs the measured Client<HttpConnector>.
The async connector uses proxy_connect_stream for secure proxy endpoints before CONNECT tunneling.
Proxy TLS uses proxy.tls_config() and ssl_new(proxy.host()), keeping proxy TLS endpoint config distinct from origin TLS.
The async connector sends CONNECT over the proxy stream and then tunnels the origin connection.

same_process_libcurl_path.evidence: The libcurl baseline is compiled under feature libcurl_bench in the benchmark binary, not executed through curl CLI.
configured_easy sets CURLOPT_URL, CURLOPT_PROXY, CURLOPT_NOSIGNAL, response body callback, and TLS environment options.
Proxy and origin TLS flags, CA files, client certs, keys, and cipher lists are mapped to libcurl options for same-scenario TLS semantics.
discard_body records libcurl body chunk and byte counts so libcurl response consumption is measured rather than skipped.
The concurrent baseline uses libcurl multi handles in the same process.
Each Easy handle installs the body stats write callback before transfer execution.

fairness_controls_observed: Benchmark client selection distinguishes ylong_http_client, ylong_http_client_sync, curl_cli, and libcurl. SOTA threshold evidence uses baseline=libcurl.
The benchmark harness records scenario, request count, repeat, warmup, concurrency, and ylong_concurrency_model in result and summary CSVs.
The latest fair S9 matrices use concurrency=8, ylong_concurrency_model=single-client, warmup=50, repeats=3, scenarios=http-over-https-proxy, https-over-https-proxy, proxy-mtls-https-origin.
Both ylong and libcurl result rows record request_body_bytes=0 and response_body_bytes=4096 for these benchmark scenarios.
Both ylong and libcurl result rows record body_chunks equal to measured request count and body_bytes equal to 4096 bytes per measured request in body-stats and later matrices.
TLS fingerprint diagnostics show ylong and libcurl negotiate TLSv1.3:TLS_AES_256_GCM_SHA384:256 on proxy TLS and origin TLS legs in the fair matrix.
Server trace counters record proxy connection counts, CONNECT counts, origin connection counts, and origin TLS connection counts, allowing same-shape checks for proxy and origin path parity.
Phase timing and TLS I/O counters are opt-in diagnostic features; canonical threshold matrices are non-phase runs unless the result directory explicitly says diagnostic.

dynamic_budget_candidate.role: retained fairness correction plus corrected TLS counter aggregation; still a SOTA counterexample

tokio_dynamic_candidate.role: runtime-substrate candidate using the same public ylong client path; best observed point estimate but still not SOTA

runtime_workers_candidate.role: rejected benchmark-runtime candidate; not retained in the benchmark binary or production client path

rejected_candidate_boundary: Explicit async HTTP/1 flush-before-response-read was tested and rejected; the candidate code and test were removed from production code.
Explicit ylong_base benchmark runtime-worker configuration was tested and rejected; the candidate code and test were removed from the benchmark binary.
OpenSSL SSL_MODE_RELEASE_BUFFERS and a dedicated nested TLS stream variant were tested and rejected; neither is retained as a SOTA claim.
This real-client-path evidence must not be read as proof that rejected benchmark-only or diagnostic-only candidates are retained.
claim_boundary: This evidence proves that current S9 benchmark evidence is generated through the real public ylong_http_client path and a same-process libcurl baseline. It does not satisfy S9 by itself, because every current SOTA threshold matrix remains a counterexample to the required numeric hard-threshold policy and final causal performance dominance is not established.
